exercises.title: Straight Arm Lat Pulldown (Cable)
exercises.notes:
Sets: 3 Working Sets.

Target: 10-15 reps @ RPE 10.

Rest: 2m 0s

Setup: Use a straight bar or rope on the highest pulley setting. Step back to create a diagonal vector. Lean forward slightly.

Vector Lock: Keep a slight micro-bend in the elbows, but lock that angle. Do not bend the arms during the movement.

The Sweep: Drive the bar in a wide arc down to your thighs. Feel the lat sweep engage before moving to the heavy compounds.

exercises.title: Pull Up (Assisted)
exercises.notes:
Sets: 2 Working Sets (Cut from 3 to reduce vertical pull redundancy).

Target: 8-10 reps @ RPE 10. (If you fall below 8, increase the machine assistance).

Rest: 3m 0s

Setup: Neutral grip (palms facing). Hands spaced roughly at skeletal shoulder width.

The Truncation: Pull yourself up, but stop just before your chin clears the bar (avoiding the final bicep squeeze). Immediately transition into the 3-second eccentric drop.

exercises.title: Iso-Lateral Row (Machine)
exercises.notes:
Sets: 3 Working Sets.

Target: 8-12 reps @ RPE 10.

Rest: 3m 0s

Setup: Neutral/vertical grip. Palms facing.

Grip: Thumbless grip is mandatory.

Mid-Range Constraint: Stop 2–3cm before the hardware reaches full contraction. Do not let the biceps hijack the load at the back of the movement.

exercises.title: Lat Pulldown (Cable)
exercises.notes:
Sets: 2 Working Sets.

Target: 10-12 reps @ RPE 10.

Rest: 2m 0s

The Stretch Bias: Because the lats will be chemically exhausted by this point, focus 100% of your energy on the 3-second eccentric stretch at the top of the movement. Let the cable pull your shoulders completely into elevation.

exercises.title: Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Machine)
exercises.notes:
Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Cable or Machine)

Sets: 3 Working Sets.

Target: 10-15 reps @ RPE 10.

Rest: 2m 0s

Scapula Lock: Push shoulder blades forward (protract) against the chest pad and lock them. Do not pinch them together at the end of the rep.

Pinky Lead: Light grip; push out with pinky fingers. Reach for the side walls.

Vector: Arms strictly parallel to the floor or angled 15–20 degrees downward. Stop when arms align with shoulders to prevent anterior roll.

exercises.title: Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Cable)
exercises.notes:
Rear Delt Reverse Fly (Cable or Machine)

Sets: 3 Working Sets.

Target: 10-15 reps @ RPE 10.

Rest: 2m 0s

Setup: Set dual pulleys slightly above shoulder height. Do not use handles; grab the rubber stoppers or the bare carabiners (removes grip/forearm bottleneck).

The Cross: Left hand holds the right cable; right hand holds the left cable. Stand perfectly upright.

Vector: Push the cables strictly outward to the side walls, angled 15–20 degrees downward.

The Stretch: On the 3-second eccentric return, allow your arms to cross completely over each other in front of your chest to violently stretch the rear delt under load.

exercises.title: Face Pull
exercises.notes:
Sets: 3 Working Sets + 1 Drop Set (Lengthened Partials).

Target: 12-15 reps @ RPE 10.

Rest: 2m 0s

Setup: Cable at height #7. Use an incline bench set at 45° for chest support. Drag bench far enough back to ensure maximum cable tension at full arm extension.

Tear: Pull toward the hairline; physically attempt to tear the rope apart.

Double Bicep: Finish with hands higher than elbows.

exercises.title: Behind the Back Curl (Cable)
exercises.notes:
Sets: 3 Working Sets (Unilateral).

Target: 8-12 reps @ RPE 10.

Rest: 1.5m 0s (Switching arms provides natural localized rest).

Setup: Bench at 45–60 degrees. Single D-handle attached to the lowest pulley.

Vector Lock: Ensure the bench is positioned so the cable pulls the arm down and slightly backward, maintaining absolute tension in the fully stretched position.

Anchor: Pin the shoulder completely flat against the pad. Do not allow the anterior deltoid to pull forward to cheat the weight up.

exercises.title: Hammer Curl (Cable)
exercises.notes:
Sets: 2 Working Sets.

Target: 10-15 reps @ RPE 10.

Rest: 1.5m 0s

Setup: Rope attachment on the lowest pulley. Step back 30-50cm from the tower to create a diagonal resistance vector.

Target Tissue: Brachialis. Pin elbows strictly to the ribcage.

Lockout: Stop the concentric pull just before the forearm stacks vertically over the bicep to maintain constant mechanical tension.

exercises.title: Cable Core Pallof Press
exercises.notes:
Sets: 3 Sets (Isometric Holds).

Target: 1 rep per set (15-20+ seconds) @ RPE 10.

Rest: 2m 0s

Setup: Seated sideways on a flat bench. Cable set to chest height. Pull handle to the exact center of the sternum and press straight out.

Anti-Slouch: "Punch" shoulder blades forward (protraction). Activate serratus.

Anchor: Brace feet heavily against the floor/machine frame. The entire lower body must act as a concrete foundation to prevent the hips from rotating.

exercises.title: Cable Core Pallof Holds
exercises.notes:
Sets: 3 Sets (Isometric Holds).

Target: 1 rep per set (15-20+ seconds) @ RPE 10.

Rest: 2m 0s

Setup: Seated sideways on a flat bench. Cable set to chest height. Pull handle to the exact center of the sternum and press straight out.

Anti-Slouch: "Punch" shoulder blades forward (protraction). Activate serratus.

Anchor: Brace feet heavily against the floor/machine frame. The entire lower body must act as a concrete foundation to prevent the hips from rotating.
